concepts.name: Graph
concepts.short_definition: A collection of nodes (vertices) connected by edges.

concepts.name: Vertex
concepts.short_definition: A fundamental unit in a graph, representing an entity.

concepts.name: Edge
concepts.short_definition: A connection between two vertices in a graph.

concepts.name: Directed Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges have a direction.

concepts.name: Undirected Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges do not have a direction.

concepts.name: Adjacency List
concepts.short_definition: A representation of a graph where each vertex has a list of its adjacent vertices.

concepts.name: Adjacency Matrix
concepts.short_definition: A representation of a graph using a matrix where entries indicate the presence of an edge.

concepts.name: Traversal
concepts.short_definition: The process of visiting each vertex in a graph exactly once.

concepts.name: Breadth-First Search
concepts.short_definition: A graph traversal algorithm that explores neighbors level by level.

concepts.name: Queue
concepts.short_definition: A data structure following the First-In, First-Out principle.

concepts.name: Visited Set
concepts.short_definition: A collection to keep track of vertices that have already been visited during traversal.

concepts.name: Starting Vertex
concepts.short_definition: The vertex from which the BFS traversal begins.

concepts.name: Neighbors
concepts.short_definition: Vertices directly connected to a given vertex by an edge.

concepts.name: Level
concepts.short_definition: The number of edges on the shortest path from the starting vertex to a given vertex.

concepts.name: Shortest Path
concepts.short_definition: The path between two vertices with the fewest edges.

concepts.name: Unweighted Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph where edges do not have associated weights.

concepts.name: BFS Algorithm Steps
concepts.short_definition: The sequential operations performed by the BFS algorithm.

concepts.name: Enqueue Operation
concepts.short_definition: Adding an element to the rear of a queue.

concepts.name: Dequeue Operation
concepts.short_definition: Removing and returning the element from the front of a queue.

concepts.name: Marking Visited
concepts.short_definition: Indicating that a vertex has been processed by the traversal algorithm.

concepts.name: Iterative BFS
concepts.short_definition: Implementing BFS using loops and explicit data structures.

concepts.name: BFS Applications
concepts.short_definition: Real-world problems and scenarios where BFS is applied.

concepts.name: Connected Components
concepts.short_definition: Subgraphs where any two vertices are connected to each other by paths.

concepts.name: Finding All Paths
concepts.short_definition: Identifying all possible sequences of edges connecting two vertices.

concepts.name: Graph Representation Choice
concepts.short_definition: Deciding between adjacency list and adjacency matrix for a graph.

concepts.name: Time Complexity of BFS
concepts.short_definition: The measure of how the execution time of BFS scales with input size.

concepts.name: Space Complexity of BFS
concepts.short_definition: The measure of how the memory usage of BFS scales with input size.

concepts.name: Tree
concepts.short_definition: A connected acyclic graph.

concepts.name: Spanning Tree
concepts.short_definition: A subgraph that includes all the vertices of the original graph and is a tree.

concepts.name: Minimum Spanning Tree
concepts.short_definition: A spanning tree of a weighted, undirected graph that has the minimum possible total edge weight.

concepts.name: Cycle Detection
concepts.short_definition: Identifying if a graph contains any cycles.

concepts.name: Bipartite Graph
concepts.short_definition: A graph whose vertices can be divided into two disjoint and independent sets.

concepts.name: Reachability
concepts.short_definition: Determining if a path exists between two vertices.

concepts.name: Graph Coloring
concepts.short_definition: Assigning colors to vertices such that no two adjacent vertices share the same color.

concepts.name: Topological Sort
concepts.short_definition: A linear ordering of vertices such that for every directed edge uv, vertex u comes before v in the ordering.

concepts.name: Web Crawling
concepts.short_definition: The process of systematically browsing the World Wide Web to create an index of documents.

concepts.name: Social Network Analysis
concepts.short_definition: Studying social structures through the use of networks and graph theory.

concepts.name: Network Routing
concepts.short_definition: The process of selecting paths in a network along which to send network traffic.

concepts.name: Game Theory
concepts.short_definition: The study of strategic decision-making.
